text: Abstract The gold(I)‐catalyzed sulfination of aryl boronic acids is presented. This initial gold chemistry report of a biologically relevant sulfination reaction exploits the previously unreported and highly relevant reactivity of the gold(I)‐heteroatom bond towards the formation of sulfinate anions. In situ subsequent reactions upon the sulfinate anions afford a wide variety of biologically relevant sulfones and sulfonamides that are widely used in medicines.
